tasks.label: Go out to the club with the friend group

memory.summary: Mina and Jae are really pushing for me to come out tonight, but I'm only 60% done with my midterm review. It feels like a huge choice between hanging out with them now or stressing about the exam tomorrow.
I just texted Mina that I'm coming over, but now my stomach is tight knowing I'm abandoning my midterm review for this night out.
The group's warmth makes the guilt of skipping my review fade, and I feel relieved to finally be here instead of worrying about Mina's disappointment.
Being welcomed by the group really eased my guilt about skipping the review; I feel relieved to be here instead of worrying about disappointing them.
The group's genuine warmth completely dissolves my lingering guilt about the skipped review; I feel entirely at ease here.
Their genuine warmth completely washes away my lingering guilt about skipping the review; I feel entirely at ease here, relieved to finally be with them.
I slept well and feel energized, but I'm still anxious that my unfinished study left me unprepared for the midterm. Being with my friends makes me forget to worry about the consequences.
The group's genuine warmth completely dissolves my lingering guilt about the skipped review; I feel entirely at ease here.
Their genuine warmth completely washes away my lingering guilt about skipping the review; I feel entirely at ease here, relieved to finally be with them.
The group's genuine warmth completely dissolves my lingering guilt about skipping the review; I feel entirely at ease here, relieved to finally be with them.
Their genuine warmth completely dissolves my lingering guilt about the skipped review; I feel entirely at ease here, relieved to finally be with them.
The club's immediate warmth erases any guilt I felt about skipping the review; being with them is all that matters right now.

desires: Stay immersed in this warm moment with Mina and Jae so I don't have to face the awkwardness of explaining why I'm still not done studying.
Let my guilt about skipping the review dissolve completely by prioritizing our plans over the professor's expectations, just like last time.
Feel that immediate relief of connection right now rather than worrying about whether tomorrow's exam will be a disaster.

goals.description: Keep the vibe alive with Mina and Jae; pivot any talk of the exam to their weekend plans so I don't have to admit I'm struggling.
goals.rationale: My affiliation value (0.5) is screaming louder than the missed deadline. Being the 'unreliable one' feels like a social threat I can't handle right now. Fixing the unfinished prep work feels too heavy and risky compared to just maintaining the warm bubble where everyone likes me.

goals.description: Make sure I'm not seen as a burden or unreliable by the group, even if it means neglecting the rest of the prep until tomorrow morning.
goals.rationale: I need to actively manage their perception. If they ask about the exam, I'll immediately change the subject to shared memories or their lives to avoid any guilt or tension. Pushing back or admitting I'm struggling feels like creating unnecessary friction.

goals.description: Find a quick way to skim the material later just enough to feel safe, then get back to hanging out without interrupting our fun.
goals.rationale: With my short planning horizon, the consequence of failing tomorrow feels distant and abstract. The immediate desire to feel at ease overrides the logic of doing the work now. However, I can't risk them thinking I've completely checked out, so I'll do a bare minimum skim later if they leave early, but only if it doesn't interrupt our vibe.

agent.last_reasoning: The exam prep is at 60% and 'soft', so the risk of failing tomorrow is low enough to ignore for now. Being here with Mina and Jae keeps my affiliation value high and avoids the friction of admitting I'm struggling later. I'll just skim whatever's left on the bus ride home.

goals.description: Reply to the group chat immediately confirming I'm coming out, even though I haven't studied the last 40% of my review yet.
goals.rationale: My affiliation value (0.5) is highest here; being excluded feels worse than failing a soft-hardness exam. Jae explicitly said 'no pressure,' which reduces my fear of awkwardness, but Mina's message ('won't be the same without you') triggers my need to avoid social friction and guilt.

goals.description: Pack my bag with just essentials so I can leave in 10 minutes if I feel panic rising about the exam.
goals.rationale: My impulsive nature means I'll likely wait until I'm already at the door or feeling the urge to study fades before doing this. It's a safety net, not a priority right now because the immediate desire is connection.

desires: Not be the one who lets Mina and Jae down by cancelling last minute; I need to feel included in their plans tonight.
Avoid feeling guilty or awkward if I have to explain why I can't go out right now.
Get some immediate relief from the stress of the exam prep without worrying about tomorrow's performance too much.
Feel connected to my friend group rather than isolated in my room studying alone.

tasks.label: Go out to the club with the friend group

action.name: message

args.content: OMG yes I'm coming! Grab me on the way!

action.reasoning: Mina's message hit hard; avoiding that guilt is my top priority right now. The exam is 'soft' and I'm 60% done, so skipping tonight feels like a small trade-off for connection. I'll text now to secure my spot, then maybe grab essentials before leaving.

trajectory.observation: Messaged Mina: 'OMG yes I'm coming! Grab me on the way!'.

goals.description: Leave immediately for the club to solidify my spot in the group and avoid any awkwardness about being late or distracted.
goals.rationale: My affiliation value (0.5) drives me to prioritize being 'seen' and present with Mina and Jae over finishing the last 40% of my review. The immediate social reward outweighs the abstract risk of a soft-hardness exam failure, especially since I've already sent the confirmation message.

goals.description: Eat a quick snack while waiting for the ride so I don't have an empty stomach ruining the night's fun.
goals.rationale: I need to manage my physical state enough to enjoy the social interaction, but this is secondary to getting out the door. My impulsive nature means I'll likely grab whatever is easiest rather than planning a full meal.

desires: Don't let Mina and Jae think I'm ditching them again; I need to be seen as loyal even if I'm half-finished with my review.
Feel the immediate rush of being in the group tonight instead of sitting alone with that tight stomach feeling about tomorrow's exam.
Avoid having a awkward conversation later where I have to explain why I didn't study more or why I'm so tired.

action.reasoning: I already messaged Mina, so leaving now avoids awkwardness. Studying helps the exam (low priority vs relationships), but skipping tonight risks my spot in the group. I'll grab a snack on the ride over; the social connection matters more than finishing this soft review.

goals.description: Stay fully immersed in the club social tonight; avoid any work talk or signs of stress that might ruin the vibe with Mina and Jae.
goals.rationale: My affiliation value (0.5) is screaming to maintain this connection. Being present and 'fun' right now is worth more than the 40% of study time I'm sacrificing. If I seem distracted, they'll notice, and that's the friction I desperately want to avoid.

goals.description: Grab a quick snack on the way back or skip it entirely if everyone else is too tired to wait, just so I don't have to plan anything complex.
goals.rationale: My impulsive nature means I'll likely eat whatever is easiest once I'm home, not before. Planning a proper meal feels like a chore that interrupts the flow of the night. I'll deal with hunger later when I can dissociate from the group dynamics.

desires: Stay immersed in the group's warmth so I don't have to face the guilt of abandoning my study time tonight.
Keep this night fun and friction-free; avoid being the one who ruins the vibe by bringing up deadlines or looking stressed.
Feel relieved that I didn't let Mina down, even though I know tomorrow's exam might be harder because I'm only 60% prepared.
